finding: Infantile Form of Hypertrophic Pyloric Stenosis

differentials.name: nonbilious projectile vomiting
differentials.raw: nonbilious projectile vomiting (sour formula / clear gastric contents) → progression over a period of several weeks after birth (15-20%)

differentials.name: palpable olive-shaped mass
differentials.raw: palpable olive-shaped mass (80% sensitive in experienced hands, up to 14% false positive)

differentials.name: Empty stomach via nasogastric tube before study
differentials.raw: Empty stomach via nasogastric tube before study

differentials.name: Remove contrast at end of study
differentials.raw: Remove contrast at end of study

differentials.name: Infantile pylorospasm =  temporary thickening of pyloric muscle
differentials.raw: Infantile pylorospasm =  temporary thickening of pyloric muscle

context: Age:     manifestation between 3rd-12th week of life; peak presentation during 4th week Frequency:   3÷1,000 births; M÷F = 4-5÷1; 5 x fold increase in 1st-degree relatives Abdominal radiograph: ◊  A normal radiograph does not exclude HPS! √  fluid-filled distended stomach after 2 hours of fasting √  little bowel gas UGI (95% sensitivity): ◊  Imaging confirmation is sought by most clinicians to differentiate from gastroesophageal reflux! Benefit:   complete anatomic evaluation of stomach and duodenum; functional assessment of gastroesophageal reflux Precautions: √  elongation + narrowing of pyl
